title: Business Idea Testing Readiness

introduction.subtitle: Maturity assessment. 7-9 min.
introduction.description:
Business Idea Testing Readiness helps you assess Assumption Mapping, Experiment Selection, Evidence Strength, Learning Decisions through concrete operating questions. Answer from observable behaviour and current evidence, not aspiration.

This educational quiz runs entirely in your browser. You receive the baseline result without account creation or email.
introduction.privacyNote: Your answers stay in this browser. The result belongs to you. Sharing is explicit and share-safe.
introduction.disclaimer: Educational diagnostic only. Not legal, tax, financial, investment, medical, employment, regulatory, or psychometric certification advice.

questions.text: How clearly are desirability, viability, feasibility, and market assumptions separated?

options.text: A. No concrete example shows separating assumption types; the answer relies on intention, memory, or individual judgement.
B. There are isolated examples of separating assumption types, but they are inconsistent, hard to verify, or depend on one person.
C. Recent examples show separating assumption types in practice, with enough detail to judge quality and repeatability.
D. Separating assumption types is repeatedly demonstrated under pressure and changes decisions, ownership, or trade-offs.
Not sure / not applicable.

conceptHelp.title: Assumption Mapping: How clearly are desirability, viability, feasibility, and market assumptions separated
conceptHelp.summary: Assumption Mapping is tested here through a concrete operating question: "How clearly are desirability, viability, feasibility, and market assumptions separated?". Use the answer choices to separate aspiration from current practice.
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Assumption Mapping matters because weak practice in this area can make the whole result look more mature than the operating system really is.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Use recent observable behaviour: artifacts, decisions, customer evidence, owners, reviews, or shipped work. Do not score intention as practice.
conceptHelp.example: A strong answer would show current evidence, a named owner, and one recent decision changed by this practice.
